purpose: Define the external review and conformance path for ISO 27001 and SOC 2 mapping work inside OpenCompliance.

reviewTracks.goal: Challenge the exact-anchor mapping edges and blocker decisions.
Challenge the source-to-atom decomposition and route assignment.
Challenge whether typed claim schemas match the intended evidence-bearing atoms.
Challenge which atoms are allowed into Lean and whether attestation and judgment boundaries are honest.
Challenge whether the public site overclaims what is proved or reviewed.

reviewerRoles.description: ISO 27001 practitioner with enough authority to challenge decomposition and control scope.
SOC 2 assurance practitioner or CPA-side reviewer who can challenge criterion and point-of-focus treatment.
Formal methods reviewer who can challenge proof boundaries and theorem attachment points.
Reviewer who can challenge control, statement, and mapping semantics in machine-readable form.
Reviewer who can challenge rights, disclosure, and legal boundary language in the public artifacts.
Implementation reviewer for evidence, fixtures, and conformance behavior.
External scrutiny role to ensure the public site remains honest about claims and limitations.

phases.goal: Ensure machine-readable status, publication model, and blocker inventory exist before requesting external time.
Review proprietary exact anchors privately where licensed material is required.
passCriteria: Anchor choices are challenged by a framework-qualified reviewer.
Public-vs-private publication decisions are recorded per anchor class.

phases.goal: Review decomposition, route, and typed claim coverage before widening the public verifier surface.
passCriteria: Decidable atoms are explicitly distinguished from attestation and judgment atoms.
Typed claim contracts exist for promoted non-judgment atoms.

phases.goal: Check that the site and public repos do not overclaim the state of mapping or proof coverage.
passCriteria: No page implies certification or CPA opinion issuance by OpenCompliance.

remainingBlockers: licensed exact-anchor review materials are still required for ISO 27001 and SOC 2
external reviewers have not yet run the program against the current blocker-aware corpus
